additional_context.value: Commercial Banking LOB digital banking platform

additional_context.source: Industry pattern for self-hosted Tier 2 banks in SEA
Industry pattern for self-hosted Tier 2 banks

data_gaps.data_needed: Specific country within Southeast Asia (affects FTE rates, regulations)
Managed Hosting fee from Backbase proposal (needed for net cost comparison)
Itemized annual infrastructure license and tooling costs
Annual unplanned downtime hours on self-hosted platform
Security team size and allocation to platform infrastructure
Annual audit and penetration testing costs
FTE disposition preference (redeployment vs. headcount reduction)
Whether platform serves multiple LOBs or commercial only

implementation_curve.description: 15-month implementation timeline (Commercial domain, medium integration complexity, SEA Tier 2 bank). Derived from ramp_up_models.md: interpolated between 12-month and 18-month rows.

effectiveness_curve.description: IT cost levers ramp faster (step-function tied to decommission). Revenue levers use commercial domain adoption discount (Y1: 0.45x, Y2: 0.65x, Y3+: 0.85x per ramp_up_models.md).

conservative.desc: Conservative scenario: 30% capture rate, slower ramp, lower adoption. Quantitative ROI is negative — the business case at this level depends on strategic and risk-reduction benefits (L4 release velocity, L6 innovation capacity, compliance de-risking) that are not quantified.

moderate.desc: Moderate scenario: 40% capture rate, standard ramp, standard adoption. Quantitative NPV is marginally negative. The business case requires strategic value (release velocity, innovation capacity, compliance risk reduction) to reach breakeven. With a lower hosting fee or multi-LOB scope, this scenario becomes NPV-positive.

aggressive.desc: Aggressive scenario: 50% capture rate, fast-track ramp, high adoption. Only scenario with positive NPV on quantified levers alone. Requires strong CIO sponsorship, rapid infrastructure decommission, and full capture of FTE/infrastructure savings.

investment.description: Managed Hosting investment: annual MH subscription fee plus one-time migration/transition costs. CRITICAL DATA GAP: hosting fee is estimated — actual Backbase proposal pricing must be substituted.
investment.notes: License = estimated annual Managed Hosting fee ($350K/yr). Implementation = migration/transition cost ($500K Y1: platform migration, environment setup, parallel run, knowledge transfer, cutover; $200K Y2: post-migration stabilization, optimization). Total 5-year investment: $2,450,000. CRITICAL: The $350K/yr hosting fee is an ESTIMATE. Actual fee from Backbase proposal will significantly change the ROI profile.

L1_D1.description: 12 FTEs currently dedicated to self-hosted platform operations (infrastructure monitoring, incident management, patching, environment management, deployment support, capacity planning). Managed Hosting absorbs these responsibilities. Bank retains 2-3 FTEs for coordination/vendor management.

gap_calculation.best_in_class: 2.5 FTEs (midpoint of 2-3 retained for coordination under Managed Hosting)
gap_calculation.gap: 9.5 FTEs = 79.2 percentage points of ops allocation
gap_calculation.impact_derivation: Gap = 12 - 2.5 = 9.5 FTEs. As % of baseline: 9.5/12 = 0.792. Capture rate 0.40: 0.792 x 0.40 = 0.317. Rounded to 0.28 to account for transition friction, knowledge retention needs, and partial FTE reallocations that are harder to capture.

L1_D1.impact_notes: Moderate: 0.28 x $873,600 = $244,608/yr steady state. Conservative (0.21): $183,456. Aggressive (0.35): $305,760.
assumptions: Loaded annual FTE cost of $72,800 based on $35/hr APAC rate x 2,080 hours
12 FTEs confirmed from engagement context (client-provided)
2-3 FTEs retained for vendor coordination (midpoint 2.5 used)
FTE redeployment framed as cost avoidance, not headcount reduction
L6 (Innovation Redeployment) excluded to prevent double-counting with same FTE pool

L2_D1.description: Self-hosted platform requires dedicated security personnel for infrastructure-layer security, annual SOC2/ISO27001 audit, and penetration testing. Managed Hosting absorbs infrastructure-layer compliance certifications. Bank retains application-level security only.

gap_calculation.best_in_class: ~$40K residual for application-level security oversight (MH absorbs infra compliance)
gap_calculation.impact_derivation: Gap ratio = 0.847. Full gap x capture: 0.847 x 0.40 = 0.339. Capped at 0.20 due to: (1) LOW confidence on security FTE allocation split, (2) uncertain residual regulatory requirements in SEA jurisdictions, (3) bank may retain higher security staffing for governance reasons.

L2_D1.impact_notes: Moderate: 0.20 x $262,000 = $52,400/yr steady state. Conservative (0.15): $39,300. Aggressive (0.25): $65,500.
assumptions: 2.5 FTEs (midpoint of 2-3) allocated to platform infrastructure security — NOT client-validated
Annual audit + pen-test costs estimated at $80K (SOC2 + ISO27001 + local regulatory)
Residual compliance effort remains for application-level security
Country-specific regulations may require additional bank-side effort — flagged as data gap

L3_D1.driver_name: Digital Transaction Revenue Protected During Avoided Downtime
L3_D1.description: Revenue protection from reduced unplanned downtime. Commercial clients conducting payments, treasury operations, and cash management via digital channels during hours that would otherwise be unavailable. Self-hosted ~99.0% uptime vs. Managed Hosting 99.95% SLA.

baseline_inputs.description: $600M x 0.40 digital x 0.05 at-risk x (87.6/2,080 hrs)

gap_calculation.client_current: ~99.0% uptime = 87.6 hours unplanned downtime/year (estimated, NOT client-validated)
gap_calculation.best_in_class: 99.95% uptime SLA = 4.38 hours downtime/year (Managed Hosting standard)
gap_calculation.gap: 83.2 hours of downtime avoided = 0.95 of current downtime
gap_calculation.impact_derivation: Gap ratio = 83.2/87.6 = 0.950. Full gap x capture: 0.950 x 0.40 = 0.380. Reduced to 0.12 because: (1) not all commercial revenue is truly lost during downtime — payments are delayed, not cancelled, (2) the 5% revenue-at-risk factor in the baseline already discounts heavily, (3) LOW confidence on actual downtime hours. The 0.12 impact is a conservative reduction from the formula-derived 0.38.

L3_D1.impact_notes: Moderate: 0.12 x $505,000 = $60,600/yr steady state. Conservative (0.09): $45,450. Aggressive (0.15): $75,750.
assumptions: Current uptime estimated at 99.0% (87.6 hrs downtime/yr) — NOT client-validated
Digital adoption rate 40% (Average benchmark from commercial/benchmarks.md: Portal Adoption 30-50%)
Revenue-at-risk factor 5% (most commercial payments delayed rather than permanently lost)
Business hours: 2,080/year (excludes after-hours batch processing)
Client retention impact from downtime not separately quantified — qualitative only

L4_D1.driver_name: Revenue Acceleration from Faster Feature Deployment
L4_D1.description: Earlier realization of revenue from new commercial banking capabilities (payment features, treasury services, portal enhancements) due to reduced deployment cycle time. Self-hosted: 4-8 week cycles. Managed Hosting: continuous delivery (days).

baseline_inputs.description: 2.5 features x 3 months delay x ($150K/12) per month

gap_calculation.client_current: Release cycle: 4-8 weeks per deployment (~6 releases/year)
gap_calculation.best_in_class: Continuous delivery: updates within days (~24+ releases/year)
gap_calculation.gap: ~18 releases/year gap; translates to ~3 months average delay per revenue feature
gap_calculation.impact_derivation: Revenue per delayed feature: $150K/yr. Each month of delay costs $12,500. 2.5 features delayed by 3 months = $93,750/yr opportunity cost. Gap-based: 0.40 capture x deployment-time-fraction: (6 wks avg current - 1 wk target) / 6 wks = 0.83 gap. 0.83 x 0.40 = 0.33. Reduced to 0.10 due to LOW confidence: feature revenue estimates are proxies, and not all deployment speed translates directly to revenue.

L4_D1.impact_notes: Moderate: 0.10 x $93,750 = $9,375/yr steady state. Conservative (0.08): $7,500. Aggressive (0.13): $12,188. Smallest quantified lever — best viewed as strategic accelerator.
assumptions: 2.5 revenue-generating features delayed per year (estimate — no client feature roadmap available)
Average delay of 3 months per feature vs. managed hosting deployment speed
Revenue per feature estimated at $150K/year (commercial portal enhancements)
This lever is best viewed as a strategic accelerator — dollar quantification is imprecise

L5_D1.description: Self-hosted platform requires server/VM licenses, OS licenses, DB licenses, monitoring tools, backup/DR software, security appliances, load balancers, and log management. Managed Hosting absorbs all infrastructure-layer costs into the MH fee.

baseline_inputs.description: Estimated $500K/yr for self-hosted platform infrastructure (midpoint of $400K-$600K range for Tier 2 SEA bank)

gap_calculation.client_current: $500K/year in infrastructure licenses and tooling (estimated — NOT client-validated)
gap_calculation.best_in_class: $0 separate infrastructure cost (fully absorbed into Managed Hosting fee)
gap_calculation.gap: $500K (100% of infrastructure cost is theoretically eliminable)
gap_calculation.impact_derivation: Not all infrastructure costs are platform-attributable — some tools serve broader bank systems. Estimated 60% directly attributable to the Backbase platform. Gap x attributable x capture: 1.00 x 0.60 x 0.40 = 0.24. MH fee captured separately in investment section — no double-counting.

L5_D1.impact_notes: Moderate: 0.24 x $500,000 = $120,000/yr steady state. Conservative (0.18): $90,000. Aggressive (0.30): $150,000.
assumptions: Annual infrastructure costs estimated at $500K — CRITICAL DATA GAP: actual itemized costs required from IT finance
60% of infrastructure costs directly attributable to the Backbase platform (rest serves other bank systems)
Includes: server/VM licenses, OS, DB licenses, monitoring, backup, DR, security appliances
Managed Hosting fee is accounted separately in the investment section — no double-counting

L7_D1.driver_name: Avoided Future Platform Upgrade & Tech Debt Remediation
L7_D1.description: Self-hosted platforms accumulate tech debt: deferred patches, version lag, security vulnerability backlog. Major remediation/upgrade every 3-5 years. Managed Hosting includes continuous platform updates, eliminating tech debt accumulation.

gap_calculation.client_current: Major platform upgrade every 3-5 years costing $500K-$1.5M; annual carrying cost ~$50K (increased incidents, workarounds)
gap_calculation.best_in_class: $0 — continuous updates included in Managed Hosting (zero tech debt accumulation)
gap_calculation.impact_derivation: Full annualized gap = $300K. Capture rate 0.40: $300K x 0.40 = $120K. As fraction of baseline: 0.40. Reduced to 0.12 because: (1) not all tech debt is infrastructure-layer (application-layer tech debt persists), (2) upgrade cost estimates are industry proxies, (3) tech debt benefits are partially realized and partially deferred.

L7_D1.impact_notes: Moderate: 0.12 x $300,000 = $36,000/yr steady state. Conservative (0.09): $27,000. Aggressive (0.15): $45,000.
assumptions: Major platform upgrade cost estimated at $1M (midpoint of $500K-$1.5M industry range)
Upgrade cycle of 4 years (typical for self-hosted banking platforms)
Annual tech debt carrying cost of $50K (increased incidents, workarounds, performance degradation)
Only infrastructure-layer tech debt counted; application-layer tech debt not addressed by MH alone
Historical upgrade costs from this bank not available — using industry pattern

qualitative_levers.lever_name: IT Staff Capacity Redeployment to Innovation
qualitative_levers.rationale_for_exclusion: Uses the same FTE pool as L1. Including both would double-count. L1 captures cost avoidance (the defensible framing). L6 captures the upside of redeploying those FTEs to revenue-generating innovation — which requires a concrete innovation pipeline to quantify.
qualitative_levers.strategic_value: 9-10 FTEs freed from ops can be redirected to digital innovation: commercial portal enhancements, API connectivity, data analytics. Shifts IT cost profile from 'run' to 'build.'
qualitative_levers.stakeholder_relevance: CIO (run vs. build rebalancing), CEO (digital transformation acceleration)

qualitative_levers.lever_name: Predictable Cost Model (CapEx to OpEx Shift)
qualitative_levers.rationale_for_exclusion: Qualitative CFO benefit — cannot be expressed as a four-link value chain with measurable volume/rate impact. Important for narrative but not quantifiable.
qualitative_levers.strategic_value: Elimination of lumpy CapEx spend on infrastructure refresh/upgrades. Replaced by predictable monthly/annual OpEx. Simplifies CFO financial planning and improves budget predictability.
qualitative_levers.stakeholder_relevance: CFO (budget predictability, CapEx reduction), CIO (simplified IT cost model)

lever_summary.name: IT Operations FTE Redeployment
lever_summary.current_state: 12 FTEs dedicated to self-hosted platform operations
lever_summary.change_driver: Managed Hosting absorbs infra ops responsibilities
lever_summary.benchmark: MH benchmark: 75-80% ops FTE reduction (industry pattern)

lever_summary.name: Compliance & Security Cost Avoidance
lever_summary.current_state: In-house SOC2/ISO27001 compliance with 2-3 security FTEs
lever_summary.change_driver: Inherited compliance certifications from Managed Hosting
lever_summary.target_state: Application-level security only; infra compliance absorbed
lever_summary.benchmark: 15-25% of IT security staff on hosting-layer security (industry)

lever_summary.name: Release Velocity & Time-to-Market
lever_summary.current_state: 4-8 week release cycles with manual deployment
lever_summary.change_driver: Continuous delivery pipeline via Managed Hosting
lever_summary.target_state: Days-not-weeks updates enabling faster feature revenue

lever_summary.name: Infrastructure License & Tooling Rationalization
lever_summary.change_driver: All infra tooling absorbed into Managed Hosting fee

lever_summary.name: Tech Debt Accumulation Avoidance
lever_summary.current_state: Tech debt accruing: deferred patches, version lag
lever_summary.change_driver: Continuous platform updates eliminate tech debt
lever_summary.target_state: Always-current platform, zero deferred upgrade backlog

assumptions_register.assumption: Loaded annual FTE cost is $72,800 ($35/hr x 2,080 hours/year)
assumptions_register.source: Engagement context — APAC commercial banking rate
assumptions_register.sensitivity: MEDIUM — L1 scales linearly; ±25% = ±$61K/yr on L1

assumptions_register.assumption: 12 IT platform FTEs dedicated to platform operations
assumptions_register.sensitivity: HIGH — primary input for largest lever (L1)

assumptions_register.assumption: 2-3 FTEs retained post-MH for coordination (midpoint 2.5 used)
assumptions_register.source: Industry pattern for managed hosting transitions
assumptions_register.sensitivity: MEDIUM — each additional retained FTE reduces L1 benefit by ~$20K/yr

assumptions_register.source: Estimate for Tier 2 commercial platform — ACTUAL PRICING REQUIRED
assumptions_register.sensitivity: CRITICAL — every $100K change in fee shifts net ROI by ~$380K over 5 years

assumptions_register.source: Estimated from typical MH migration projects
assumptions_register.sensitivity: MEDIUM — one-time cost, impacts Y1-Y2 cash flow

assumptions_register.source: Industry estimate for self-hosted Tier 2 bank platform ($400K-$600K range)
assumptions_register.sensitivity: HIGH — L5 is a significant lever; ±$100K changes benefit by ±$24K/yr

assumptions_register.source: Industry pattern for self-hosted Tier 2 SEA banks
assumptions_register.sensitivity: MEDIUM — if actual uptime is 99.5%, L3 benefit halves

assumptions_register.assumption: Digital adoption rate 40% of commercial clients
L6 (IT Innovation Redeployment) excluded to avoid double-counting with L1
assumptions_register.source: Lever candidates flagged L1/L6 as sharing same FTE pool

assumptions_register.assumption: Implementation timeline: 15 months for Managed Hosting migration
assumptions_register.source: Derived from ramp_up_models.md (commercial domain, medium complexity)
assumptions_register.sensitivity: MEDIUM — affects timing of benefits but not steady-state magnitude

assumptions_register.assumption: Platform serves commercial LOB only (not multi-LOB)
assumptions_register.sensitivity: HIGH — if multi-LOB, L1/L2/L5 benefits increase proportionally

data_gaps_for_validation.item: Actual Managed Hosting fee from Backbase proposal
data_gaps_for_validation.impacts: Total investment, net ROI, payback period — single most important data gap

data_gaps_for_validation.item: Itemized annual infrastructure license and tooling costs
Annual unplanned downtime hours on self-hosted platform
Security team allocation to platform infrastructure

data_gaps_for_validation.impacts: FTE cost rates, regulatory requirements, benchmark applicability
data_gaps_for_validation.current_assumption: Generic SEA — using APAC average rates

data_gaps_for_validation.item: FTE disposition preference (redeployment vs. headcount reduction)
data_gaps_for_validation.impacts: L1 framing (cost avoidance vs. cost reduction) and L6 inclusion/exclusion
Multiplier on L1, L2, L5 if platform serves multiple business lines
